title: Return to al-Wagidi 's Account of the Two Battles Mentioned Above
description: Mentioned Above The Syrians set out under Mu'awiyah b. Abi Sufyan, while 'Abdallah b. Sa'd b. Abi Sarh was in command of the men from the maritime districts (ahl al-balir).127 According to (al-Wagidi): In that same year, Constantine the son of Heraclius128 set forth, because of what the Muslims had done to them (that is, the Byzantines] in Africa, in a fleet (jam`) whose like had not been assembled by the Byzantines since the coming of Islam. They set out in five hundred ships and encountered (the Muslims) under 'Abdallah b. Sa'd.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 15
